description: 'howai' is the later part of the day as daylight settles and ordinary daytime activity gives way to the night. It includes the social and practical span around sunset; 'norawhi' (dusk) names the fading light itself.
articulatory_notes: Open the glottis for unvoiced 'h' and round into middle-height back 'o'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then open into stressed central 'a'. Keep the final high-front 'i' separate by releasing the rounding and lifting the front of the tongue across the hiatus.

semantic_domains.nature: settling daylight around the day's transition towards darkness
semantic_domains.temporal: the late daily span after afternoon and before night
